rules.name: readme-read-before-edit-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: readme-read-before-edit-write
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: dev-watch-before-source-edit
rules.evidence: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running).

rules.name: bootstrap-build-after-branch-switch
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches

rules.name: bootstrap-build-before-ci-push
rules.evidence: Use full `pnpm build-all` for branch switches/bootstrap, before CI push, or when changes span multiple packages.

rules.name: no-test-rerun-with-different-filters
rules.evidence: Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters. Capture output once to a file, then read from it

rules.name: rebuild-before-integration-tests
rules.evidence: When running Next.js integration tests, you must rebuild if source files have changed

rules.name: no-settimeout-in-tests-edit
rules.evidence: Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting

rules.name: no-settimeout-in-tests-write
rules.evidence: Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-in-tests-edit
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-in-tests-write
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-over-inline-files-edit
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-over-inline-files-write
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: use-new-test-generator-mandatory
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: gh-pr-create-fork-branch-policy
rules.evidence: You cannot write full descriptions for fork PRs where the merge target is `vercel/next.js`.

rules.name: gh-pr-create-llm-comment-required
rules.evidence: When writing full PR/Issue/Discussion descriptions, commit messages, or leaving comments on pull requests, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description or comment: <!-- NEXT_JS_LLM -->

rules.name: gh-issue-create-membership-gate
rules.evidence: only members of the `vercel` or `vercel-labs` GitHub organizations may use an agent to create issues, discussions, or leave comments

rules.name: no-ai-footer-in-commits
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: commit-message-concise
rules.evidence: Keep commit messages concise and descriptive

rules.name: pr-description-focus
rules.evidence: PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why

rules.name: no-mark-pr-ready-for-review
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: pre-validate-before-commit
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each)

rules.name: never-commit-secret-files
rules.evidence: Never commit local secret files; if documenting env setup, use placeholder-only examples.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-force-push-rebase
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: flags-type-schema-defineenv-edit
rules.evidence: New flags: add type in `config-shared.ts`, schema in `config-schema.ts`, and `define-env.ts` when used in user-bundled code.

rules.name: flags-runtime-env-wiring-edit
rules.evidence: If a flag is consumed in pre-compiled runtime internals, also wire runtime env values (`next-server.ts`/`export/worker.ts` as needed).

rules.name: defineenv-edge-force-false-edit
rules.evidence: In edge builds, force feature flags that gate Node-only imports to `false` in `define-env.ts`.

rules.name: require-behind-compiletime-branch-dce-edit
rules.evidence: Keep `require()` behind compile-time `if/else` branches for DCE (avoid early-return/throw patterns).

rules.name: require-behind-compiletime-branch-dce-write
rules.evidence: Keep `require()` behind compile-time `if/else` branches for DCE (avoid early-return/throw patterns).

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-entry-base-only-edit
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: react-server-dom-webpack-entry-base-only-write
rules.evidence: `react-server-dom-webpack/*` imports must stay in `entry-base.ts`; consume via component module exports elsewhere.

rules.name: app-page-no-relative-require-edit
rules.evidence: You cannot require internal modules with relative paths because they won't be resolvable from the user's project.

rules.name: mode-specific-test-skipstart-edit
rules.evidence: Mode-specific tests need `skipStart: true` + manual `next.start()` in `beforeAll` after mode check

rules.name: mode-specific-test-skipstart-write
rules.evidence: Mode-specific tests need `skipStart: true` + manual `next.start()` in `beforeAll` after mode check

rules.name: no-browser-back-in-act-tests-edit
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: no-browser-back-in-act-tests-write
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: rust-avoid-super-imports-edit
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: rust-avoid-super-imports-write
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: docs-highlight-line-numbers-accuracy-edit
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block ... Double-check highlights by counting lines from 1 within each code block.

rules.name: docs-highlight-line-numbers-accuracy-write
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block ... Double-check highlights by counting lines from 1 within each code block.

rules.name: flag-non-standard-header-read-security-edit
rules.evidence: if new code reads a request header that is not a standard HTTP header (like `content-type`, `accept`, `user-agent`, `host`, `authorization`, `cookie`, etc.), flag it for security review.

rules.name: flag-non-standard-header-read-security-write
rules.evidence: if new code reads a request header that is not a standard HTTP header (like `content-type`, `accept`, `user-agent`, `host`, `authorization`, `cookie`, etc.), flag it for security review.

skill_candidates.summary: Fast local development workflow: start the watch build in the background, run the test command matching dev/prod x turbopack/webpack mode, then stop the watch process when finished.
skill_candidates.evidence: 1. Start watch build in background: ... 2. Run focused tests with the matching mode script: ... 3. When done, kill the background watch process (if you started it).

skill_candidates.summary: Quick smoke-testing with a throwaway app: generate a minimal fixture via the test generator, boot the dev server directly on a port, then curl it for fast feedback without the full test harness.
skill_candidates.evidence: generate a minimal test fixture with `pnpm new-test -- --args true <name> e2e`, then run the dev server directly with `node packages/next/dist/bin/next dev --port <port>` and `curl --max-time 10`.

always_on: CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md — they are the same file.
Monorepo layout and key package/directory reference (packages/next, turbopack/, crates/, test/, examples/, docs/, scripts/, and the Key Directories quick-reference list).

Build command reference (pnpm --filter=next build, pnpm build, pnpm build-all, pnpm --filter=next exec taskr) and when to prefer the type-check-only pnpm --filter=next types shortcut over a full build.
Turbopack is the default bundler for next dev/next build; use --webpack to force webpack, and there is no --no-turbopack flag.
Test command reference by mode (test-dev-turbo/webpack, test-start-turbo/webpack, test-unit) and pnpm new-test for interactively generating a new test file.
Linting/type command reference (pnpm lint, lint-fix, prettier-fix, types).

PR-status triage priorities: prioritize blocking failures (build/lint/types before tests), treat failures as real unless disproven, reproduce with the same CI env vars (e.g. IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1), and use the matching mode-specific test command for module-resolution/build-graph fixes; full workflow lives in the $pr-status-triage skill.

CI analysis tips: prioritize CI failures over review comments and blocking jobs (build/lint/types before tests); quick fixes for common fast-check failures (cargo fmt for rust check, prettier --write for lint failures).

Development tips: dev server entry point and router server file locations; use DEBUG=next:* for debug logging and NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 for local testing; use process.env.__NEXT_DEV_SERVER (not just NODE_ENV) to distinguish next dev from next build --debug-prerender / next start.

Secrets/env handling: treat all env values as sensitive unless known test-mode flags; never print/paste secret values in chat, commits, or logs; mirror CI env names/modes without inlining literal secret values; if a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholders; redact sensitive-looking values when sharing command output.

GitHub SSH auth troubleshooting: on SSH signing/agent errors (sign_and_send_pubkey, communication with agent failed, Permission denied), stop and ask the user to unlock/provide their SSH agent or key provider rather than switching remotes to HTTPS, mutating remote URLs, or retrying repeatedly.

Specialized skills exist for conditional/deep workflows (pr-status-triage, create-pr, backport-pr, flags, dce-edge, react-vendoring, react-sync, runtime-debug, next-rspack, authoring-skills); baseline iteration/build/test policy stays in this file.

Context-efficient workflows: grep large files (>500 lines) for line numbers before reading targeted ranges, never re-read an unchanged section, search-only (don't read) generated files like dist/ or node_modules/, capture build/test output to a file once rather than re-running, batch edits before rebuilding, and save external API responses to a file/variable instead of re-fetching.

Task decomposition and verification methodology: split work into independently verifiable steps, verify each step before moving to the next using the right method (tests/types/lint/build/manual inspection), and ask the user when there's no obvious verification method.

Test-writing gotchas: cache-components/PPR testing notes (test with __NEXT_CACHE_COMPONENTS=true rather than the mostly-skipped ppr-full/ppr suites); don't rely on exact log message text — filter by content patterns and sequences, not positions; snapshot tests vary by CI env flags, so update them using the exact env flags the CI job uses; reproduce CI failures locally with the exact CI env vars (e.g. IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1); set __NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED=true to see full stack traces instead of collapsed ignore-listed frames.
Rust/Cargo notes: cargo fmt uses ASCII sort order; on an internal compiler error (ICE), delete the */incremental directories under the cargo target dir and retry.

Node.js source map notes: findSourceMap() needs --enable-source-maps or it returns undefined; source map path formats vary between webpack and tsc; process.cwd() varies between test and production stack-trace formatting.

Stale native binary: if Turbopack errors look wrong after switching branches or pulling, delete packages/next-swc/native/*.node and run pnpm install to get the published binary instead of a stale local build.
define-env.ts affects user bundling only — it does not control pre-compiled runtime bundle internals.

Development-anti-patterns pointers: reproduce module-resolution/bundling issues with the normal mode-specific test command; validate edge bundling regressions with pnpm test-start-webpack test/e2e/app-dir/app/standalone.test.ts; use __NEXT_SHOW_IGNORE_LISTED=true for full internal stack traces when debugging framework internals.
